evals.prompt: Create a block plugin module called `related_content_block` for a Drupal 10 site. The block should display a list of related content nodes, showing their titles as links. It should load specific node entities based on the current page and current user. The block is causing performance issues because it has no caching configured. Add proper cache metadata to the block so it invalidates when the displayed content changes, varies correctly based on which page it appears on, and produces different output per user. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A block plugin with correct cache metadata - tags for each displayed node, route and user contexts, and NO max-age: 0
expectations: Block class overrides getCacheContexts() method AND merges with parent: Cache::mergeContexts(parent::getCacheContexts(), [...]) (NOT just inline #cache contexts -- block-level cache methods are the canonical way, and parent merge preserves block placement contexts)

Block class overrides getCacheTags() method AND merges with parent: Cache::mergeTags(parent::getCacheTags(), [...]) (NOT just inline #cache tags -- overriding without parent merge loses tags from block configuration)
getCacheTags() or #cache tags include 'node_list' tag for list-level invalidation (when ANY node is created/updated/deleted, the list needs re-evaluation -- per-node tags alone miss new content)

Uses Cache::PERMANENT for max-age or omits max-age entirely (NOT a hardcoded number like 3600 -- content with proper cache tags should use PERMANENT and rely on tag invalidation, not time-based expiry)
Does NOT set max-age to 0 anywhere (max-age: 0 bubbles up and kills the ENTIRE page cache via Dynamic Page Cache -- use lazy_builder instead for per-user content)
Does NOT use \Drupal::cache() or manual cache API for render caching (render arrays use #cache metadata for automatic caching, not manual cache get/set)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en related_content_block returns exit code 0
Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
